instruction.global: Throw a red die from the top right to strike the right side of a 2x2 stack of dice, knocking the right column away while leaving the left column standing.
steps: Introduce a red die falling from the top right toward the stack with visible motion blur.
Depict the falling die colliding with the right side of the stack, causing the two rightmost dice to tumble and scatter to the right.
Move the scattered dice further to the right as they lose momentum, with the foreground die becoming out of focus.

explain: A die enters the frame from above, gaining velocity due to gravity and exhibiting motion blur.
Kinetic energy is transferred from the falling die to the right half of the stack upon impact, displacing those dice.
The displaced dice continue to slide and roll across the surface until friction brings them to a rest at new coordinates.
invariants: Static camera viewpoint
Plain white background and reflective surface
Consistent lighting and shadows
The left column of two stacked dice remains undisturbed

instruction.global: Collapse the tower of white blocks into a scattered pile on the floor, revealing a hidden sphere at the center.
steps: Destabilize the tower so the blocks on the right side begin to fall and scatter at the base.
Complete the collapse of the structure until all blocks form a disorganized heap.
Settle the pile further, spreading the blocks outward to reveal a white sphere resting in the center.

explain: Gravity and a loss of structural integrity cause the outer blocks to tumble downward and outward.
The remaining vertical support fails, leading to a total collapse where potential energy is converted into kinetic energy and then rest.
The blocks settle into a flatter distribution due to friction and momentum, uncovering an object previously obscured by the tower.
invariants: Static camera viewpoint
Solid black background
Reflective black floor surface
White material and rectangular shape of the blocks
Consistent studio lighting

instruction.global: Make the front-most stack of coins tip over and fall into the collection bin while keeping the other two stacks stable.
steps: Tilt the front-most stack of coins forward toward the drop, showing it losing its balance while still mostly intact.
Collapse the stack completely, scattering the coins in mid-air with motion blur as they fall toward the lower tray.
Remove the falling coins and the front stack entirely, leaving only the scattered coins on the tray and the two original stacks in the background.

explain: Gravity causes the center of mass of the leaning stack to shift past its base, initiating a rotational fall.
The stack loses structural cohesion during the fall, causing individual coins to separate and disperse due to kinetic energy.
The coins complete their trajectory, settling on the lower platform or falling into the prize chute out of frame.
invariants: The two coin stacks in the background must remain stationary and unchanged.
The camera viewpoint and angle must stay fixed.
The green machine housing and 'Coin Tickets' signage must remain consistent.
The lighting and reflections on the glass must be preserved.

instruction.global: Place a red owl mug onto the triple beam balance and prepare to adjust the sliding weights.
steps: Lower the red owl mug onto the balance pan and release it, causing the beam to tilt.
Move the hand away from the mug toward the bottom left of the frame.
Bring the hand back toward the center to point at the sliding weights on the balance beam.

explain: The weight of the mug is applied to the pan, causing the left side of the balance to drop and the beam to tip upward on the right.
The hand is withdrawn, leaving the mug's mass as the sole force acting on the scale's pan.
The hand returns to the mechanism to begin the process of sliding the poises to find the object's mass.
invariants: camera viewpoint
background and table surface
appearance of the red owl mug
lighting conditions
triple beam balance structure

instruction.global: The large stack of pink and yellow poker chips collapses forward into the coin pusher tray, scattering the existing coins and revealing a black pouch.
steps: Make the large stack of pink chips tip over and fall toward the camera with significant motion blur.
Show the falling chips impacting the tray, scattering the silver coins and orange stacks across the surface.
Settle the chips into a final resting position, revealing a black velvet pouch amidst the yellow chips and showing the silver coins pushed to the back.

explain: Gravity overcomes the stability of the high-piled stack, causing it to collapse forward.
The kinetic energy from the falling chips is transferred to the items in the tray, causing a chain reaction of displacement.
Friction and collisions bring the objects to a rest, resulting in a new distribution of chips and coins based on the impact.
invariants: Static camera viewpoint
Machine interior walls and glass reflections
Specific chip denominations and colors ($5000 pink, $1000 yellow, $10000 orange)
Consistent lighting from the top of the machine

instruction.global: A black ball rolls through the base of two wooden block towers, causing the entire structure to collapse into a scattered pile.
steps: Introduce a black ball hitting the bottom of the left tower, causing the tower to buckle and the connecting bridge to break.
Show the left tower completely collapsing while the right tower tilts and begins to fall as the ball passes through.
Depict all wooden blocks settled in a flat pile on the ground with the black ball having rolled to the far right.

explain: The kinetic energy of the moving ball is transferred to the base blocks, overcoming their static friction and destabilizing the vertical equilibrium of the tower.
Gravity pulls the unsupported upper blocks downward, creating a cascading collapse, while the ball's momentum continues to disrupt the second tower's foundation.
The potential energy of the standing towers is fully converted into kinetic energy and then dissipated as the blocks reach a stable state on the floor.
invariants: Camera viewpoint and focal length
Grey background and white floor surface
Wood grain texture and dimensions of the blocks
Lighting direction and soft shadows
Material properties of the black ball

instruction.global: Collapse the standing Jenga tower so that the blocks are scattered across the table and the person's hand is removed from the scene.
steps: Topple the wooden tower completely, scattering the blocks across the right side of the table surface and removing the hand.
Maintain the scattered blocks in their fallen positions on the table.
Keep the arrangement of the fallen blocks consistent with the previous state.

explain: The removal of a support block causes the tower to lose structural integrity, leading to a gravitational collapse where blocks scatter upon impact with the table.
The blocks reach a state of static equilibrium, resting motionless on the flat surface after the kinetic energy from the fall dissipates.
The system remains at rest as no external forces are applied to the scattered blocks.
invariants: Camera viewpoint and angle
Wooden table texture and grain pattern
Solid black background
Lighting and shadows on the table
The specific 'Jenga' branding and wood color of the blocks

instruction.global: Show the dominoes falling and knocking over the Jenga tower until it collapses completely.
steps: Tip the line of dominoes so they strike the base of the wooden tower, causing the tower to begin leaning.
Increase the tilt of the wooden tower as the momentum from the base impact travels upward, causing the top figure to start falling.
Complete the collapse of the tower, scattering all the wooden blocks across the table surface.

explain: Kinetic energy from the falling dominoes is transferred to the base of the tower, destabilizing its center of gravity.
As the base shifts, the gravitational torque increases, causing the entire structure to lean and accelerate downwards.
The structural integrity fails completely, and the blocks fall under gravity, dispersing upon impact with the table.
invariants: Camera viewpoint and angle
Background wall color and texture
Table surface material and lighting
The appearance and material of the wooden blocks
